learningObjectives.title: Understand resource master data
learningObjectives.description: Explain resources as physical assets used for transportation execution.

learningObjectives.title: Understand resource assignment
learningObjectives.description: Describe capacity checks and assignment flow during transportation planning.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate resource and carrier
learningObjectives.description: Clarify asset-based execution versus business-partner service provider role.

sections.title: Resource Master Data in SAP TM

sections.content: Resources represent the physical assets used to execute transportation. They include trucks, trailers, containers, rail wagons, ships, aircraft and even drivers. During transportation planning, SAP TM assigns available resources to Freight Orders to execute shipments efficiently.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: A Resource is an executable transportation asset. Freight Orders cannot be executed until suitable resources are assigned.

SAP TM evaluates resource capacity before assignment. Vehicle weight limits, volume limits, loading restrictions, calendars and availability are checked to ensure the selected resource can execute the shipment.

A shipment weighing 18 tons is generated from Hyderabad to Chennai. SAP TM evaluates available trucks, compares vehicle capacities and assigns a 20-ton truck because it satisfies both weight and scheduling requirements.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain accurate vehicle capacities, calendars and availability to improve optimizer decisions and prevent overloading.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics company maintained 1,200 trucks and 2,800 trailers in SAP TM. Automatic capacity-based resource assignment increased vehicle utilization by 18% and reduced empty trips.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: Resource = Physical Asset, Means of Transport = Transportation Mode. This distinction is commonly tested in SAP TM certification exams.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Resources with Carriers. A Carrier is a Business Partner providing transportation services, while a Resource is the physical asset used to perform transportation.

items: Resources are assigned during transportation planning.
Capacity and availability are checked before assignment.
Resources support planning, execution and optimization.
Carrier and Resource are different master data objects.
